summary: Mexico's economy is estimated to have grown 1.7% in June, with services leading the gain while investment stayed the main drag ahead of INEGI's formal second-quarter figures. In the private sector, FEMSA is pushing its Spin fintech unit to become a digital version of its Oxxo network, aiming to match Oxxo's scale as it competes with digital banks Nu and Revolut in Mexico.

lead.h1: Mexico's economy seen growing 1.7% in June, led by services
lead.context: Services drove the gain while investment remained the main drag, according to early estimates ahead of INEGI's formal second-quarter data.
lead.background: INEGI's IOAE is an early, unofficial estimate of economic activity published ahead of the formal quarterly GDP report, used to gauge momentum before official data lands.
lead.drivers: May's estimated growth of 1.6% and April's 2.2% feed into a projected 1.8% for the second quarter, well above the first quarter's 0.3%.
lead.implications: The acceleration would mark the strongest quarterly pace in about two years and lift first-half growth to 1.1% from 0.4% a year earlier.

coverage.title: Mexico's economy seen growing 1.7% in June, led by services
coverage.summary: Services drove the gain while investment remained the main drag, according to early estimates ahead of INEGI's formal second-quarter data.

items.headline: FEMSA pushes Spin to become a digital Oxxo to rival Nu and Revolut
items.context: FEMSA's leadership wants its Spin fintech unit to replicate the scale of its Oxxo convenience-store network in digital finance, positioning it against fast-growing digital banks operating in Mexico.
items.background: FEMSA operates Oxxo, Mexico's largest convenience-store chain with more than 24,000 stores, alongside Spin, a fintech launched in 2021 that grew separately until early 2026.
items.drivers: FEMSA leadership told Spin's head to 'do another Oxxo digitally,' pushing the firm to merge Spin, Oxxo and credit into one financial system able to match Nu, Revolut and Mercado Pago.
items.implications: The push targets credit, a segment García Jacques says neither neobanks nor traditional banks have solved.

coverage.title: FEMSA pushes Spin to become a digital Oxxo to rival Nu and Revolut
coverage.summary: FEMSA's leadership wants its Spin fintech unit to replicate the scale of its Oxxo convenience-store network in digital finance, positioning it against fast-growing digital banks operating in Mexico.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.40 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.
